tasks.title: Add explicit WROTE/NOT-WRITTEN verdict to attribution-tracer summary
tasks.description: Patch claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md so the agent's final output is honest about whether the learning record was written. After the artifact-grounding-judge returns its verdict, the attribution-tracer must echo the judge's raw verdict JSON, then terminate with exactly one of: 'WROTE: <id>' (judge verdict pass AND log-learning.py exited 0) or 'NOT WRITTEN: <reason>' (judge verdict rejected, log-learning.py errored, or any earlier failure). The current behavior allows the summary to say 'written' regardless of outcome. This is the Layer 3 fix (summary honesty).
acceptance_criteria: attribution-tracer.md contains an explicit output contract section requiring the agent to end its summary with 'WROTE: <id>' or 'NOT WRITTEN: <reason>'
The output contract specifies that the judge's raw verdict JSON must be echoed before the terminal line
The output contract covers all three failure cases: verdict rejected, log-learning.py non-zero exit, and input validation failure (pre-emptive for T-0038)
A reviewer can confirm the change by reading attribution-tracer.md without running the agent

tasks.title: Add transcript_path input validation to attribution-tracer
tasks.description: Patch claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md to add a Step 0 (before the existing Step 1 transcript read) that validates transcript_path. If transcript_path is absent from the input prompt or if the file at that path does not exist on disk, the agent must immediately return {"error": "transcript_path missing or unreadable: <path>"} and stop. It must not proceed to draft a record, draft evidence, or spawn the grounding judge. This is the Layer 2 fix (fail-fast on bad input). The terminal output rule from T-0037 applies: this exit path must emit 'NOT WRITTEN: transcript_path missing or unreadable'.
acceptance_criteria: attribution-tracer.md contains a Step 0 input validation block before Step 1
Step 0 specifies the exact error JSON to return when transcript_path is absent or unreadable
Step 0 specifies that the agent stops immediately — no record draft, no grounding judge spawn
The error path emits the NOT WRITTEN terminal line per the T-0037 output contract
A reviewer can confirm the change by reading attribution-tracer.md without running the agent

tasks.title: Add transcript_path resolution to debug skill attribution-capture block
tasks.description: Patch claude-code-shared/skills/debug/SKILL.md's attribution-capture block to derive and pass transcript_path explicitly before spawning the attribution-tracer agent. The current block says transcript_path is 'always available' but provides no mechanism to obtain it. Add a concrete bash command using $CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR env vars (the same mechanism filter-session-transcript.sh uses) to resolve the absolute path to the current session JSONL file. Require that the resolved path be passed explicitly in the agent spawn prompt under the transcript_path key. This is the Layer 1 fix (transcript path derivation at the call site).
acceptance_criteria: debug/SKILL.md attribution-capture block contains a concrete bash command to resolve transcript_path using $CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR before spawning the attribution-tracer
The block explicitly states that the resolved path must be passed in the agent prompt under the transcript_path key
The vague 'always available' language is replaced with the concrete resolution mechanism
A reviewer can confirm the change by reading debug/SKILL.md without running the skill
tasks.pr:
gh pr create --title "Fix attribution pipeline false positives" --body "$(cat <<EOF
### Fix attribution pipeline false positives

The attribution-tracer silently claimed success when the grounding judge rejected a record or log-learning.py errored. The debug skill also had no mechanism to resolve transcript_path, causing silent empty-path failures.

**Changes:**
- attribution-tracer: Add Step 0 input validation (fail-fast on missing/unreadable transcript_path)
- attribution-tracer: Add terminal output rule requiring WROTE/<id> or NOT WRITTEN/<reason> after judge verdict
- debug skill: Replace vague "always available" transcript_path note with concrete bash derivation using CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
EOF
)"

follow_ups.title: Re-run attribution trace for the lost SpawnedSapien invalidateQueries learning
steps: Switch to the SpawnedSapien project directory: cd /Users/eric/Documents/dev/personal/SpawnedSapien

Open a new Claude Code session in SpawnedSapien and invoke /debug with the confirmed issue: 'useRefundOrderItem.ts never called queryClient.invalidateQueries after success, causing item.refundedAt to stay null until page reload. Fix: void queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["orderHistory"] }) after setStatus("done") in useRefundOrderItem.ts. Root criterion: T-0004 acceptance criterion "On success: Order Item shows refunded state without full page reload" was underspecified.'
Verify the attribution-tracer summary ends with 'WROTE: <id>' (not a fabricated success claim)
